2605.06346.title: Prediction and Empowerment: A Theory of Agency through Bridge Interfaces
2605.06346.paper_summary: We study agency under partial observability in deterministic physical or simulated worlds, where apparent randomness arises from uncertainty over initial conditions, fixed law bits, and unrolled exogenous noise. We model sensing and actuation as bridge interfaces split between agent-controlled parameters and environment-controlled channel state, inducing a deterministic POMDP through a prior over latent microstates and many-to-one observation coarsening. Within this framework, we prove a separation between prediction, compression, and empowerment. Perfect prediction can be achieved either by identifying the hidden quotient relevant to the target family or by overwrite control that makes the future target action-determined; high empowerment alone is insufficient. Under refinable interfaces and sufficient memory, action-conditioned observation-compression progress reduces posterior uncertainty about the latent quotient, and when refinement requires steering world-side channel conditions, this creates target-conditioned interface empowerment. A bit-string specialization with a conserved information budget makes the resulting tradeoff explicit: prediction by identification requires internal capacity at least the relevant latent entropy, whereas overwrite control requires terminal action capacity over the controlled quotient. For modern AI agents, the results suggest a design principle rather than a theorem of inevitability: objectives should distinguish hidden-state identification, interface refinement, task-relevant controllability, and mere overwrite or distractor control. Human--AI alignment is partly an interface-design problem, where the relevant bridge is between human intent, agent internal state, external tools, and world-side channel conditions. This is a working draft: feedback and criticism is most welcome.
2605.06346.paper_summary_zh: 我們研究在部分可觀察性的情況下的代理行為，這些情況發生在確定性的物理或模擬世界中，其中表面隨機性源於對初始條件、固定法則位元和展開的外生噪聲的不確定性。我們將感知和執行建模為在代理控制的參數和環境控制的通道狀態之間分割的橋接介面，通過對潛在微狀態的先驗和多對一觀察粗化來誘導確定性POMDP。在這個框架內，我們證明了預測、壓縮和賦能之間的分離。完美的預測可以通過識別與目標家族相關的隱藏商或通過覆寫控制來實現，後者使未來的目標行動變得確定；僅僅高賦能是不夠的。在可細化的介面和足夠的記憶下，基於行動的觀察壓縮進展減少了對潛在商的後驗不確定性，當細化需要引導世界側通道條件時，這會創造目標條件下的介面賦能。一種具有保守信息預算的位元串專門化使得結果的權衡變得明確：通過識別進行預測需要的內部容量至少是相關的潛在熵，而覆寫控制則需要對受控商的終端行動容量。對於現代AI代理，這些結果暗示了一種設計原則，而非必然的定理：目標應該區分隱藏狀態識別、介面細化、任務相關的可控性以及單純的覆寫或干擾控制。人類與AI的對齊在某種程度上是一個介面設計問題，其中相關的橋接在於人類意圖、代理內部狀態、外部工具和世界側通道條件之間。這是一份工作草稿：歡迎反饋和批評。
2605.06346.author: Richard Csaky et.al.
